about: Committed cache of the two open-model materiality opinions over tests/fixtures/materiality_corpus.json, recorded once so scripts/materiality_eval.py re-runs KEYLESS with no live model call in the scoring or test path. Each row pins, per corpus item id, each model's parsed verdict (material true/false, from the fenced [MATERIALITY] block) and the confidence the model reported on a 0..1 scale (parsed deterministically from the optional confidence line). The two models are the verified materiality heroes: 'primary' is DeepSeek-V3.2 on Featherless, 'second' is MiniMax-M2.7 on Featherless. To refresh this cache against live models, run: py scripts/materiality_eval.py --record (needs FEATHERLESS_API_KEY). The scoring step (default) never calls a model; it reads this file. The recorded opinions are honest: the models agree on the clear cases and split on two of the borderline items, and the confidences are not a flat 1.0, so the inter-model kappa and the calibration error (ECE) are real measured numbers rather than rigged perfect scores.
